channel.title: Pulse
channel.description: Curated podcast episodes by Pulse — current awareness radar. Episodes are organized by tracked topic.
channel.itunes:author: TARS
channel.itunes:summary: Curated podcast episodes by Pulse — current awareness radar. Episodes are organized by tracked topic.

item.title: [iran-war] — Conflict spreads to the wider region after the US-Israel strikes on Iran
item.description: The US and Israel’s campaign against Iran, that resulted in the death of Tehran’s Supreme Leader Khamenei, has now expanded into a regional conflict. Monocle’s Gulf correspondent Inzamam Rashid brings us the latest as strikes have now been seen in
item.itunes:author: The Globalist

item.title: [iran-war] — 297. Operation Ajax: The 1953 CIA Plot to Topple Iran (and Other Background Notes)
item.description: Is Iran at an historic turning point? Iranian-born Alex Vatanka of the Middle East Institute weighs in on the likely paths and consequences. We also discuss the US history of intervention in Iran dating back to the CIA’s Operation Ajax in 1953.\nOrder
item.itunes:author: The Sharyl Attkisson Podcast

item.title: [iran-war] — A Debate with Dave Smith: Israel, Iran, and American Power
item.description:
Note that this conversation took place before Hamas addressed some conditions of President Donald Trump’s proposed peace plan and said it agreed to release all remaining hostages.



This was the most requested conversation I've ever had, and one
item.itunes:author: Conversations with Coleman

item.title: [iran-war] — Global Hotspots: U.S. Weighs Direct Involvement in Israel/Iran Conflict
item.description: This week, Israel escalated its military campaign against Iran’s nuclear and military infrastructure, while the U.S. weighed potential involvement in the conflict. In the Indo-Pacific, Taiwan added Chinese companies to its export blacklist, limiting t
item.itunes:author: New Lines Institute for Strategy and Policy | Podcasts

item.title: [iran-war] — Gulf states caught in the middle of US-Iran conflict
item.description: Investors turned to gold and the US dollar in the wake of the Iran conflict, and Gulf states are panicking as Iran targets them in retaliatory strikes. Plus,can the US economy handle the surging oil prices caused by the attacks? \nMentioned in this podc
item.itunes:author: FT News Briefing

item.title: [iran-war] — EP: 435 | Israel-Iran Conflict Analysis with Experts Jason Baidya & Deepak Gyawali
item.description: Dive deep into the Israel-Iran conflict with geopolitical experts Jason Baidya and Deepak Gyawali in this comprehensive podcast episode. Explore the complex dynamics of the Middle East unrest, including the recent escalation, US aid, and the strategic r
item.itunes:author: Sushant Pradhan Podcast

item.title: [iran-war] — Wargaming WW3: how and where the next global conflict could actually unfold
item.description: From Taiwan to Estonia and Latvia, the prospect of World War Three feels closer than ever - that is unless you're one of those people who thinks it's already begun.\nPeter Apps, Reuters' Global Defence Commentator, is not one of those people, but he does
item.itunes:author: Iran: The Latest

item.title: [iran-war] — EI Weekly Listen — Kimberly Kagan on the United States and the new way of war
item.description: The United States, still the dominant military power in the world, is immersed in a new era of warfare that it has not yet recognised as endemic and enduring. America is losing its wars to less powerful but more adaptable adversaries, while preparing inadequately for future inter-state conflicts. Re
item.itunes:author: The EI Podcast

item.title: [iran-war] — U.S.-Iran Conflict, Part 2: The Iranian model of asymmetric warfare
item.description: A big part of America's fear of Iran arises from the idea that Iran sponsors various militant groups in the region, Hezbollah being a prominent example.
item.itunes:author: In Focus by The Hindu

item.title: [iran-war] — Capt. Matt Hoh : Is U.S. Military Policy Being Driven by Israel?
item.description: Capt. Matt Hoh : Is U.S. Military Policy Being Driven by Israel?\n\nSee Privacy Policy at https://art19.com/privacy and California Privacy Notice at https://art19.com/privacy#do-not-sell-my-info.
item.itunes:author: Judging Freedom

item.title: [iran-war] — Is the U.S. going to war with Iran?
item.description: Features national security and White House correspondents breaking down the shifting administration goals and the emerging domestic political friction over launching a regime-change war without congressional approval.
item.itunes:author: The NPR Politics Podcast

item.title: [iran-war] — A New Era of Conflict - Understanding the U.S. Strategy Against Iran and Its Consequences
item.description: Provides a deep-dive analysis into the specific military strategy behind the U.S. strikes, offering a hawkish expert perspective on why a protracted, multi-domain campaign is viewed as a necessary strategic reset despite international backlash.
item.itunes:author: John Solomon Reports

item.title: [iran-war] — Edmund Fitton-Brown and Bill Roggio examine Iran's potential responses to US military pressure, contrasting diplomatic signals with threats of offensive missile deployment and regional proxy warfare.
item.description: Edmund Fitton-Brown and Bill Roggio examine Iran's potential responses to US military pressure, contrasting diplomatic signals with threats of offensive missile deployment and regional proxy warfare. 15.1843 RUSSIAN EMBASSY TO PERSIA
item.itunes:author: The John Batchelor Show

item.title: [iran-war] — US-Iran conflict: Short- and long-term risks
item.description: Following the recent killings and retaliatory str…
item.itunes:author: The Decisive Podcast

item.title: [iran-war] — ‘Our Existence Is Not Up For Debate’: Israeli Ambassador To US On Iran, US Relations...
item.description: As Iran tensions spike and U.S. evacuations from Israel begin, how close are we to another regional war? \n\nIn our latest podcast, Mosheh sits down with Israel’s Ambassador to the United States, Yechiel Leiter, for an urgent, wide-ranging conversation
item.itunes:author: Mo News

item.title: [iran-war] — The Other Iran-Israel War
item.description: In addition to the three-dimensional war of bombs and missiles, Iran and Israel—and the US—are squaring off in a cyber battle. With everything from water infrastructure to banking connected to the internet in some way, how much damage can cyberattacks do? \nGuest: Maggie Miller, POLITICO cybersecuri
item.itunes:author: What Next | Daily News and Analysis

item.title: [iran-war] — Is the U.S. headed toward military conflict with Iran?
item.description:
New York Times journalist David Sanger discusses how we got here, the state of Iran's nuclear weapons program, the likelihood of U.S. military force against Iran and if Trump's goal is regime change.

To manage podcast ad preferences, review the links below:

See pcm.adswizz.com for information abou
item.itunes:author: Fresh Air

item.title: [iran-war] — Experts Feared a Wider War. It's Finally Here
item.description: We’re in a “history defining moment that will shape the Middle East for years to come.”
item.itunes:author: What Next | Daily News and Analysis

item.title: [OpenClaw] — Is OpenClaw’s Hype Real? Are AI Agents Replacing Jobs? What’s the Anti-AI Thesis?
item.description: The Napkin Math duo is back with another episode. We spend most of this one trying to make sense of AI agents, OpenClaw - the new kid on the block, and why this wave feels messier but more real than the last few. We talk through hype vs reality, what agents can actually do today, and how work is quietly shifting from roles and seats to tasks and systems. We also discuss AI fluency levels, how people are using Claude Code in real marketing workflows, one-person companies, and end with the Anti-AI portfolio. Timestamps 00:00 Intro 01:15 OpenClaw & the agent breakout moment 06:52 Hype vs. reality: shiny tools, boring winners 09:45 The Dropbox lesson: abstraction beats raw tech 12:43 AI agents, security & where this breaks 19:07 AI vs SaaS, leverage & outcome-based work 24:58 Are AI agents a
item.itunes:author: Napkin Math

item.title: [OpenClaw] — Moltbook 3:16 – DTNS Live 5113
item.description:
We share our feelings about Moltbook, a Reddit-like site for AI agents from popular AI assistant platform OpenClaw. Komei asked what steps any of us have taken to make provisions for our accounts after we die. And the Information reports Nvidia will not be introducing any new GPUs in 2026. Is this the final nail in the coffin for the DIY PC enthusiast hobby?
Starring Sarah Lane, Tom Merritt, Robb Dunewood, Len Peralta, Roger Chang, Joe.
To read the show notes click here!
Support the show on Patreon by becoming a supporter!
item.itunes:author: Daily Tech News Show (VIDEO)

item.title: [OpenClaw] — OpenClaw & Moltbook: AI Agents and Cybersecurity Risks
item.description:
Autonomous AI assistants are hitting the mainstream — but at what cost? This week, we discuss the recent OpenClaw phenomenon (formerly Clawdbot/Moltbot), the security fiasco surrounding Moltbook’s exposed database, and the quirky yet concerning AI agent dating platform MoltMatch. We explore the privacy and cybersecurity implications of entrusting AI agents with sensitive access and how defenders should think about emerging agentic risks.
** Links mentioned on the show **
OpenClaw (a.k.a. Moltbot) is everywhere all at once, and a disaster waiting to happen
https://garymarcus.substack.com/p/openclaw-aka-moltbot-is-everywhere
Exposed Moltbook Database Let Anyone Take Control of Any AI Agent on the Site
https://www.404media.co/exposed-moltbook-database-let-anyone-take-control-of-any-ai-
item.itunes:author: Shared Security Podcast

item.title: [Iran-US Conflict] — BONUS: What does the Iran conflict mean for the global economy?
item.description: Roughly a quarter of the world's oil supply is at risk as the biggest military strike in decades unfolds in the Middle East. Rory Johnston is a geopolitical analyst who writes the Substack Commodity Context. He talks to Paul Haavardsrud about how this conflict play out for Canadian crude and the global economy.
item.itunes:author: Cost of Living

item.title: [Iran-US Conflict] — What Is a War?
item.description:
The Trump administration’s attacks on Venezuelan boats defy even the broadest interpretation of the president's war powers.
Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.
item.itunes:author: What Trump Can Teach Us About Con Law

item.title: [openclaw] — [openclaw] Podcast: OpenClaw is the first real “keyboard test” for agentic AI and most teams aren’t ready.
item.itunes:author: drstorm.substack.com

item.title: [openclaw] — [openclaw] The OpenClaw-ification of AI - The AI Daily Brief
item.itunes:author: The AI Daily Brief

item.title: [Israel-US vs Iran] — Iran’s leadership, retaliation, travel and oil disruption and Trump’s plan
item.description:
Israel has launched a new wave of strikes on Tehran after killing Iran's supreme leader and leaving the Islamic Republic grappling to rebuild its leadership. The U.S. and Israeli strikes, and Iranian retaliation, have sent shockwaves through sectors from shipping to air travel to oil. Inside Iran, some grieved for Khamenei while others have celebrated his death. Protests against the military action have also erupted across Pakistan and Iraq.
Follow the ⁠latest developments in Iran live⁠ by subs
item.itunes:author: Reuters World News

item.title: [Israel-US vs Iran] — Breaking news podcast: Inside the high-stakes U.S.-Israel attack on Iran | Haaretz defense analyst Amos Harel
item.description: In this special edition of the Haaretz Podcast, recorded during the first hours of the dramatic joint U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran, and as Tehran began its retaliatory strikes on Israel and on U.S. targets across the Middle East, Haaretz senior analyst Amos Harel joins host Allison Kaplan Sommer for a real-time update and discussion. "The stakes are much higher than last time," Harel said, referring to the 12 day Israel-Iran war in June 2025. For Israelis, "there is a certain amount of dange
item.itunes:author: Haaretz
